name: Jitbit Helpdesk API
what_it_does: Helpdesk ticketing system with REST API available as both cloud SaaS and self-hosted on-premise — built on ASP.NET, supporting email-based ticket creation, agent management, and integration via REST API and webhooks.
use_cases: Creating and updating support tickets from external systems via REST API
Querying ticket status and customer history for CRM integration agents
Self-hosted helpdesk integration where data must stay on-premise
Automating ticket assignment and escalation via API-triggered rules
Syncing helpdesk data with Active Directory and enterprise systems
not_for: Modern multi-channel helpdesk with social media and chat (Jitbit is email-focused)
Large teams needing enterprise SLA complexity and advanced analytics
Teams preferring open-source (Jitbit is proprietary, self-hosted option is licensed)
best_when: A Windows-centric IT team needs on-premise helpdesk with REST API, Active Directory integration, and familiar ASP.NET deployment.
avoid_when: You need open-source, modern UI with live chat, or Linux-native deployment.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with agent credentials, or API token from account settings. Bearer token or Basic auth header. Self-hosted instances use same auth mechanism. Admin can generate tokens per agent.

pricing.paid_tiers: SaaS: Freelancer $29/month; Startup $69; Company $129; Enterprise $249. Self-hosted license from $1,699 one-time
pricing.notes: Self-hosted option with one-time license fee — good for long-term cost control. SaaS pricing per account not per agent. On-premise version requires Windows Server. Proven product since 2006.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits — varies by plan and deployment

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers tickets, users, categories, and company management. Webhook support for ticket events. API key auth available. Self-hosted API may differ from cloud — verify version.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ticket creation creates new tickets. Update operations are idempotent. Read operations always idempotent. No explicit idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted and cloud API may differ — test against your specific deployment
Basic auth with agent credentials ties API access to human accounts
Windows Server required for self-hosted — Linux environments need alternative
Webhook configuration requires UI setup — cannot be configured via API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on SaaS; configurable self-hosted. Basic auth or token. GDPR compliant. Self-hosted offers full data control. Proprietary software — no public security audits.
